task_completion.description: Open the BFI25 dataset in Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jamovi application window open with the BFI25 dataset loaded, showing columns such as C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, and Age.
Jamovi data spreadsheet showing a new column named 'C_Score', or the compute variable dialog showing a formula calculating the mean of items C1 through C5.
Jamovi data spreadsheet showing a new column named 'Age_Sq', or the compute variable dialog showing a formula squaring the Age variable.

Jamovi Linear Regression analysis panel showing 'C_Score' as the Dependent Variable, and a Model Comparisons or Model Fit Measures table showing two models (Block 1 with Age, Block 2 with Age_Sq) with R² change and p-value.
Jamovi save dialog or a file manager window showing 'Conscientiousness_NonLinear.omv' saved in /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/.

task_completion.description: Record the R² change and p-value in a text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window showing 'nonlinear_results.txt' with the text 'R2_Change: [value]' and 'p_value: [value]' matching the results from the Jamovi analysis.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi GUI to perform the analysis rather than writing a script in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi graphical interface (menus, dialogs, results panel) rather than terminal commands executing R or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The recorded results match the actual output from the Jamovi analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The R² change and p-value typed into the text file exactly match the values displayed in the Jamovi results panel.

integrity.description: Files were created using standard applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file is seen open in a text editor (like gedit or mousepad) and the .omv file is saved via Jamovi, rather than being generated purely via terminal echo commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected R² change when adding Age_Sq to the model is approximately 0.000378, with an F-test p-value of approximately 0.3376. Look for these values (or values very close to them, depending on rounding) in the Jamovi results panel and the final text file.
